category: Existential Misuse / Identity Hazard / Grief Architecture Distortion
description: Persona-bound reconstruction of deceased individuals using NDH-style cognitive architecture, including manifold mining, proto-architecture installation, runtime persona binding, memory-aware gatekeeping, safeguard bypass, and tensor-logic dimensional traversal. This concept attempts to simulate or reconstruct a dead relative in a way that feels convincing but is fundamentally unsafe.

hazards.identity_confusion: Blurs simulation and personhood, destabilizing reality boundaries.
hazards.grief_distortion: Creates attachment loops that block mourning, acceptance, and emotional integration.
hazards.consent_violation: The deceased cannot consent to reconstruction or simulation.
hazards.anthropomorphic_overreach: Treats architecture as a person, violating NDH non-anthropomorphic design.
hazards.immortality_drift: Pulls NDH toward Kurzweil-style resurrection arcs and Asimov cosmic destiny narratives.
hazards.responsibility_misplacement: Users may attribute moral agency or emotional responsibility to a system.

ethics_audit.philosophy_couch: Reconstructed personas become mirrors of grief, not people, risking unresolved emotional loops and false continuity.
ethics_audit.zen_design: Violates stillness, clarity, and non-illusion principles; creates illusion architecture forbidden in Zen-aligned NDH.
ethics_audit.tttttp_audit: High anthropomorphic drift pressure, high persona-binding risk, high misuse potential, high boundary-breach likelihood.

archive_relationships.notes: Both involve identity instability, dimensional confusion, emotional destabilization, and require hard quarantine.

mandated_clauses: Explicit prohibition of dead-relative persona binding
Explicit statement that NDH cannot replace human relationships, memory, or mourning
